mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness forces wage increases that compress corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and retail sales indicates demand-pull overheating that forces central banks to tighten policy, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive shocks to CPIAUCSL and RSAFS yield positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.04 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals expanding corporate output capacity and improving supply chain conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of observations during recovery regimes, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, inflation expectations breach the central bank tolerance threshold, prompting market pricing of imminent rate hikes that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns in > 50% of samples, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 across all rolling test windows.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with near-zero policy rates indicate strong residential investment demand and cheap financing, which amplifies economic momentum and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply bottlenecks and margin compression, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP-INDPRO divergence predicts positive 60-day returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 in walk-forward validation.
